231.title: Proširi odgovor: tvrdnja + razlog + primjer
231.intro: Examiners reward answers of three or more sentences, so never stop at a bare 'da' or 'ne'. Train one reflex for every speaking prompt: state your point (tvrdnja), give a reason (razlog), then add an example (primjer). These twelve ready-made frames turn a one-word answer into a confident mini-paragraph. Say each aloud with your own content and the expanding becomes automatic on exam day.

items.note: The three-step answer. Every prompt gets: what I think, why I think it, and one concrete example. Three sentences, minimum.

items.hr: Slažem se s tim, i to zato što…
items.en: I agree with that, and that's because…
items.note: The bridge from tvrdnja to razlog: i to zato što + full clause. zato što = because (with a verb).
The signal for your primjer. Primjerice is the slightly more formal variant — perfect for the exam register.
A strong way to open an example: Iz vlastitog iskustva, padeži su najteži dio. vlastiti = one's own.
A lighter, conversational way to introduce an example: Recimo, jučer sam… Keeps you talking while you think.
Adds weight to a point: Učim ne samo gramatiku nego i izgovor. Both parts take the same case/structure.

items.hr: Ono što mi je najvažnije jest…
items.en: What matters most to me is…
items.note: A confident opener that raises the register. Follow with a noun or a da-clause: …jest da razumijem obitelj.
Buys you a sentence and shows control: Da to pojasnim, mislim na svakodnevne razgovore. pojasniti = to clarify.

items.en: When I think about it a bit more…
items.note: A natural way to extend an answer and add a second reason instead of falling silent.
Links your example back to your point: …i baš zato volim hrvatski. Closes the loop neatly.

items.en: In short, my position is clear.
items.note: A tidy closing frame after you've given reason and example. Ukratko = in short; stav = stance, position.

items.hr: Volim planinariti jer me opušta; na primjer, subotom idem na Sljeme.
items.en: I like hiking because it relaxes me; for example, on Saturdays I go to Sljeme.
items.note: The full pattern in one line: tvrdnja (Volim planinariti) + razlog (jer me opušta) + primjer (subotom idem na Sljeme).

231.title: Expanding every answer to 3+ sentences
231.intro: Drill the machinery of the long answer: the connector that opens a reason, the phrase that flags an example, and the frames that let you keep talking. Build the reflex tvrdnja → razlog → primjer before the simulation.

questions.prompt: What does 'Na primjer' signal in an answer?
questions.explanation: Na primjer / Primjerice = for example — it introduces the primjer, the third step of the expanded answer.

questions.prompt: Which three-step order do examiners reward?
questions.explanation: tvrdnja → razlog → primjer: say your point, justify it, then give a concrete example.

questions.prompt: Complete the reason link: Slažem se, i to zato ______ je hrvatski koristan. ('because')
questions.explanation: zato ŠTO + verb = because. It introduces the razlog: i to zato što je hrvatski koristan.

questions.prompt: Which frame best opens a personal example?
questions.explanation: Iz vlastitog iskustva = from my own experience — a strong lead-in to a concrete example.

questions.prompt: Complete: Ne ______ gramatiku nego i izgovor. ('not only')
questions.explanation: ne samo… nego i… = not only… but also… : Ne učim samo gramatiku nego i izgovor.

questions.prompt: Complete the tidy closer: '______, moj je stav jasan.'
questions.explanation: Ukratko = in short — a closing frame after you've given a reason and an example.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'What matters most to me is the family.'
questions.answer: Ono što mi je najvažnije jest obitelj
questions.explanation: Relative frame Ono što mi je najvažnije + jest + the noun obitelj. The clitics mi je sit in second position of the relative clause.

questions.prompt: Build a full three-step answer: 'I like hiking because it relaxes me; for example, I go to Sljeme.'
questions.answer: Volim planinariti jer me opušta na primjer, idem na Sljeme
questions.explanation: tvrdnja (Volim planinariti) + razlog (jer me opušta) + primjer (na primjer, idem na Sljeme). The whole pattern in one breath.

questions.prompt: You are asked 'Volite li hrvatski?'. Which reply best fits the exam?
options: Volim ga jer zvuči lijepo; na primjer, uživam u pjesmama Olivera Dragojevića.
questions.answer: Volim ga jer zvuči lijepo; na primjer, uživam u pjesmama Olivera Dragojevića.
questions.explanation: Only the third answer gives statement + reason + example — three sentences of content, exactly what examiners reward.

231.title: Simulacija govorenja #2
231.intro: Your second full oral simulation: your Croatian wife plays the examiner and works through the mock exam speaking prompts. Read your lines ('Me') aloud and hold the rule — every answer gets at least three sentences: statement, reason, example. Notice how calm and long the answers feel compared with day 224.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dobar dan. Za početak, predstavite se i recite nešto o sebi.
lines.en: Good afternoon. To begin, introduce yourself and tell me something about yourself.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dobar dan. Zovem se Ricardo i živim u Hrvatskoj već nekoliko godina. Učim hrvatski jer mi je žena Hrvatica, a i djecu želimo odgajati dvojezično. Na primjer, kod kuće svaki dan pola sata razgovaramo samo na hrvatskom.
lines.en: Good afternoon. My name is Ricardo and I've lived in Croatia for a few years now. I'm learning Croatian because my wife is Croatian, and we also want to raise our children bilingually. For example, at home we talk only in Croatian for half an hour every day.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vrlo lijepo. Recite mi, kako obično provodite vikend?
lines.en: Very nice. Tell me, how do you usually spend the weekend?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vikend najviše volim provoditi u prirodi jer me to opušta nakon napornog tjedna. Iz vlastitog iskustva, šetnja uz more djeluje bolje od bilo kojeg lijeka. Recimo, prošle subote smo žena i ja pješačili tri sata i vratili se sretni i umorni.
lines.en: I most love spending the weekend in nature, because it relaxes me after a hard week. From my own experience, a walk by the sea works better than any medicine. Let's say, last Saturday my wife and I hiked for three hours and came back happy and tired.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Slažete li se da je danas teže odgajati djecu nego prije?
lines.en: Do you agree that it's harder to raise children today than before?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Djelomično se slažem, i to zato što djeca danas odrastaju s ekranima. S jedne strane imaju više prilika za učenje, a s druge strane teže se koncentriraju. Da to pojasnim: moje dijete brzo nauči nove riječi na tabletu, ali teško sjedi mirno uz knjigu.
lines.en: I partly agree, and that's because children today grow up with screens. On the one hand they have more learning opportunities, and on the other they concentrate less easily. To clarify: my child quickly learns new words on a tablet, but has a hard time sitting still with a book.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Odlično ste to razvili. Za kraj, što ćete raditi kad položite ispit?
lines.en: You developed that excellently. Lastly, what will you do when you pass the exam?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Kad položim ispit, prvo ću proslaviti s obitelji. Ono što mi je najvažnije jest da ću se moći slobodno sporazumijevati na hrvatskom. Baš zato nastavljam učiti i poslije ispita — jezik se ne staje voljeti.
lines.en: When I pass the exam, first I'll celebrate with my family. What matters most to me is that I'll be able to communicate freely in Croatian. That's exactly why I'll keep learning even after the exam — you don't stop loving a language.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bravo! Odgovori su bili puno duži i mirniji nego prošli put. Spreman si.
lines.en: Bravo! Your answers were much longer and calmer than last time. You're ready.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Hvala. Osjećam razliku — sada mi rečenice dolaze same od sebe.
lines.en: Thank you. I feel the difference — now the sentences come to me on their own.

232.title: Izdržljivost u slušanju: pratiti duži prilog
232.intro: Exam audio will feel easy only if a full HRT bulletin feels normal. Endurance listening means holding attention across a whole segment and catching one fact per story: the number, the place, the time, the decision. These twelve signpost phrases mark the turns of a longer report so you never lose the thread — and so you know where the key fact is about to land.

items.en: in the introduction / at the start
items.note: The anchor previews the stories here. Listen for the topics, not yet the details.

items.en: as we learn / according to what we hear
items.note: A reporting hedge that usually precedes a fresh fact: Kako doznajemo, radovi počinju sutra.

items.en: on the other hand / by contrast
items.note: Signals a turn to a contrasting fact — often a TOČNO/NETOČNO trap hides right here.
The anchor repeats background you may have missed. A second chance at a key detail.
Introduces a forecast or plan: Očekuje se više od tisuću posjetitelja. Watch for the number after it.
prema + dative; the number or statistic follows immediately — mark it down.
A hand-off to a new story. Reset your ear for a new topic, place and fact.
javljati = to report in; dopisnik = correspondent. Cues a field report with concrete on-the-ground detail.
A round-up of short items — several facts back to back. Attention must stay high.
što se tiče + genitive: introduces the topic of the next fact. Što se tiče vremena, sutra slijedi zahladnjenje.

items.en: To finish, here is the weather forecast.
items.note: The standard close of a bulletin. If you hear it, the last facts are the forecast — temperatures and conditions.
A cue before a break; the bulletin continues afterward. Endurance means not switching off here.

232.title: One fact per story
232.intro: Exam-style listening under load: several passes over longer items, each time pulling out the single fact that answers the question — the number, the place, the decision. The text stays hidden; you may replay twice, exactly like the real test.

questions.prompt: What does 'očekuje se' signal you should listen for next?
questions.explanation: očekuje se = it is expected — a forecast or figure follows: Očekuje se više od tisuću posjetitelja.

questions.prompt: Listen. How many people are expected at the festival?
questions.explanation: The report says očekuje se više od pet tisuća posjetitelja = more than five thousand visitors are expected.
questions.audioText: Ovogodišnji filmski festival u Puli počinje u petak. Prema posljednjim podacima organizatora, očekuje se više od pet tisuća posjetitelja iz cijele regije.

questions.prompt: Listen. Why will the motorway be closed tonight?
questions.explanation: Kako doznajemo, dionica autoceste bit će zatvorena zbog radova = the section will be closed because of roadworks.
questions.audioText: Kako doznajemo, dionica autoceste između Zagreba i Karlovca bit će noćas zatvorena zbog radova, od ponoći do pet sati ujutro.

questions.prompt: From the same report: until what time is the road closed? (upišite broj)
questions.explanation: od ponoći do pet sati ujutro = from midnight until five in the morning. The endurance point: hold both the reason and the second number.

questions.prompt: What does the phrase 'što se tiče' introduce?
options: the topic of the next fact
questions.answer: the topic of the next fact
questions.explanation: što se tiče + genitive = as for / regarding — it flags the subject of the fact that follows.

questions.prompt: Listen. What is the main change in the weather for tomorrow?
questions.explanation: Što se tiče vremena, sutra slijedi zahladnjenje uz kišu = a cooling with rain follows tomorrow.
questions.audioText: Za kraj donosimo vremensku prognozu. Što se tiče vremena, sutra na cijelom području slijedi osjetno zahladnjenje uz kišu i jači sjeverni vjetar.

questions.prompt: Complete the reporting phrase: Kako ______, radovi počinju u ponedjeljak. ('as we learn')
questions.explanation: Kako doznajemo = as we learn / hear — a hedge that usually precedes a fresh fact.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'To finish, here is the weather forecast.'
questions.explanation: za kraj = to finish; verb donosimo + object vremensku prognozu (accusative). The standard close of a bulletin.

233.title: Brzina čitanja: skeniraj prije nego čitaš
233.intro: Slow reading steals time from writing. The exam reward goes to whoever scans first and reads second: names, dates and numbers jump out before you read a single full sentence, so you know where each answer lives. These twelve words and reading moves build the habit — scan for the landmark, then read only the sentence around it, and finish each task with a quarter of its time to spare.

items.note: Reading fast for the gist. First pass over any text: what is it about, and how is it laid out?
The question tells you the keyword; find it in the text, then read only around it. Names and numbers are the easiest anchors.

items.en: a piece of information, a datum
items.note: podaci = data. Exam questions hunt for one podatak — a date, a price, a place. Scan for its shape, not its meaning.
Read the naslov first — it frames the whole text and often predicts the questions.
Each odlomak usually holds one main idea. Locate the paragraph, then read it closely.
The exam's favourite question stem: answer only from what is written, never from your own knowledge.
The TOČNO/NETOČNO items — find the exact sentence that confirms or contradicts, and beware one changed word.
Se koji se ne spominje u tekstu…: 'which is NOT mentioned' — a common trap wording. Scan to confirm presence or absence.
After scanning, form a one-line sažetak in your head. If you can summarise, you understood enough to answer.
The spare time is for this: reread the sentence, confirm the podatak matches. Target 25% of the clock left for checking.

items.hr: Imena, datumi i brojevi prvi upadaju u oči.
items.en: Names, dates and numbers jump out first.
items.note: The scanning motto. Capital letters and digits are visual landmarks — use them to jump straight to the answer.

items.hr: Prvo skeniraj, pa tek onda pažljivo čitaj.
items.en: First scan, only then read carefully.
items.note: The two-pass method that saves the exam clock. Never read word by word from the top.

233.title: Scan, locate, answer — against the clock
233.intro: Practise the two-pass method on a short exam-style text: scan for the landmark, read only the sentence around it, answer from the text. The last items lock in the reading vocabulary that frames every task.

questions.prompt: What should you do on your FIRST pass over an exam text?
options: skim for the gist and layout
questions.answer: skim for the gist and layout
questions.explanation: letimično čitanje first: get the topic and structure, then scan for keywords. Never read word by word from the top.

questions.prompt: Which are the easiest landmarks to scan for?
questions.explanation: Imena, datumi i brojevi prvi upadaju u oči — capitals and digits are visual anchors that lead you straight to the answer.

questions.prompt: Read: 'Knjižnica Marka Marulića u Splitu radi radnim danom od 8 do 20 sati, a subotom od 9 do 14 sati. Nedjeljom je zatvorena. Učlanjenje stoji pet eura godišnje.' — Until what time is the library open on weekdays?
questions.explanation: Scan for the weekday hours: 'radnim danom od 8 do 20 sati'. The Saturday hours (do 14) are the distractor.

questions.prompt: From the same text: how much does membership cost per year? (upišite broj u eurima)
questions.explanation: 'Učlanjenje stoji pet eura godišnje.' Scan for the number, then confirm the sentence around it.

questions.prompt: From the same text — TOČNO ili NETOČNO: Knjižnica radi nedjeljom.
questions.explanation: 'Nedjeljom je zatvorena' — closed on Sundays. Find the exact sentence; beware answering from habit.

questions.prompt: The question says 'prema tekstu'. What does that instruct you to do?
options: answer only from what the text says
questions.answer: answer only from what the text says
questions.explanation: prema tekstu = according to the text — the answer must come from the passage, never from outside knowledge.

questions.prompt: Put the reading motto in order: 'First scan, only then read carefully.'
questions.answer: Prvo skeniraj, pa tek onda pažljivo čitaj
questions.explanation: Prvo skeniraj (first scan), pa tek onda (only then) pažljivo čitaj (read carefully) — the two-pass method.

questions.prompt: A question reads: 'Što se u tekstu NE spominje?'. What is it testing?
options: which detail is absent from the text
questions.answer: which detail is absent from the text
questions.explanation: ne spominje se = is not mentioned. Scan each option against the text; the answer is the one you cannot find.

234.title: Završni prolaz kroz gramatiku: neka bude dosadna
234.intro: The night before the mock, your grammar must be boring — automatic, not thought about. This is the exam's grammar catalogue in one place: genitive plurals after numbers, the conditional bih/bismo, the spelling of futur I (ići ćemo vs radit ću), prefixed aspect pairs, clitic order, and the passive. Read each with strict diacritics and drill anything that still makes you pause.

items.note: Numbers 5+ and all vague quantities take the GENITIVE PLURAL: pet godina, mnogo ljudi, nekoliko kuća. The classic exam trap.
2-4 take the genitive SINGULAR (dva sata, tri godine, četiri čovjeka); 5+ switch to genitive plural (pet sati, pet godina).

items.hr: bih, bi, bi, bismo, biste, bi
items.note: kondicional I = bih + participle. Watch the plural: mi BISMO, vi BISTE — colloquial 'bi' for all persons is not standard.
Verbs in -ći keep the FULL infinitive before the auxiliary: ići ću, doći ćeš, reći ćemo. Never contract them.
Only -ti verbs drop the -i and write the auxiliary SEPARATELY: radit ću, pisat ćeš, kuhat ćemo. Two words, no -i.
Aspect pair: čitati = the process; pročitati = read the whole thing to the end. Choose by whether the action is completed.
The prefix perfectivises: pisati → napisati (write it), raditi → uraditi/napraviti (get it done). Completed = perfective.
Clitic order: dative (mi) → accusative (ga) → aux 'je' LAST. je always closes the cluster: Dao mi ga je.
Order after li: li → aux (si) is skipped as 'jesi li' fronts, then pronoun ga: Jesi li ga vidio? li comes right after the first word.
Passive with the participle: biti + trpni pridjev (sagrađena). Agrees in gender/number: kuća je sagrađena, pismo je napisano.
The se-passive: reflexive se + verb, no named agent. Ovdje se govori hrvatski = Croatian is spoken here.

items.hr: Kad bih imao vremena, putovao bih.
items.en: If I had time, I would travel.
items.note: The conditional sentence: kad bih + participle in the if-clause, bih + participle in the main clause. Male: imao/putovao; female: imala/putovala.

234.title: Grammar catalogue — strict diacritics
234.intro: A last full pass over the exam's grammar: genitive after numbers, the conditional forms, futur I spelling, aspect in context, clitic order and the passive. Diacritics count on every item — mark yourself strictly.

questions.prompt: Hrvatska ima oko četiri milijuna ______.
questions.explanation: milijun and numbers above 4 take the genitive plural: stanovnika.

questions.prompt: Čekam te već ______. (three hours)
questions.explanation: 2-4 take the genitive SINGULAR: tri sata. Only 5+ switch to genitive plural (pet sati).

questions.prompt: Write futur I correctly: 'We will go together.' — Zajedno ______ (ići, mi).
questions.explanation: -ći verbs keep the full infinitive before the auxiliary: ići ćemo — two words, never *ićemo.

questions.prompt: Write futur I correctly: 'I will work tomorrow.' — Sutra ______ (raditi, ja).
questions.explanation: -ti verbs drop the -i and write the auxiliary separately: radit ću — two words, no -i, no fused *radiću.

questions.prompt: I mi ______ rado došli na proslavu!
questions.explanation: 1st person plural conditional is bismo. Colloquial 'bi' for all persons is not standard on the exam.

questions.prompt: Choose the aspect: 'I read the whole book yesterday.' — Jučer sam ______ cijelu knjigu.
questions.explanation: A completed whole action → perfective pročitati: pročitao sam. čitao sam would mean 'was reading' (process).

questions.prompt: Correct clitic order: 'He gave it to me.'
questions.explanation: Order: dative mi → accusative ga → aux je LAST. 'je' always closes the cluster: Dao mi ga je.

questions.prompt: Passive: 'The letter was written yesterday.' — Pismo je jučer ______.
questions.explanation: Passive participle agrees with pismo (neuter): pismo je napisano. Masculine would be napisan, feminine napisana.

questions.prompt: Put the conditional sentence in order: 'If I had time, I would travel.'
questions.answer: Kad bih imao vremena, putovao bih
questions.explanation: kad bih + participle (imao) in the if-clause; putovao bih in the main clause. Both halves carry bih.

235.title: Strategija za mock #2: kako izvući svaki bod
235.intro: Today is the dress rehearsal — the full mock in the Quiz tab, all five sections, timed, one sitting, real conditions. This is the go/no-go signal: at least 60% in listening, reading and grammar plus a pass in writing and speaking means you can book the real exam. Before you start, fix the strategy for each section so no easy point slips away.

items.hr: Slušanje: bilježi na prvom slušanju, provjeri na drugom.
items.en: Listening: note on the first play, verify on the second.
items.note: You hear each item twice. First pass: catch the fact. Second pass: confirm the number, place or TOČNO/NETOČNO.

items.hr: Čitanje: prvo skeniraj, pa čitaj oko ključne riječi.
items.en: Reading: scan first, then read around the keyword.
items.note: Names, dates and numbers first. Answer only prema tekstu — from the text, not from what you know.

items.hr: Gramatika: pazi na dijakritike i padež iza prijedloga.
items.en: Grammar: mind diacritics and the case after each preposition.
items.note: č/ć/š/ž/đ count. u/na + locative for place, s + instrumental for company, od/do + genitive.

items.hr: Pisanje: nacrt, prebroji rečenice, pa lektura.
items.en: Writing: draft, count sentences, then proofread.
items.note: 7-8 sentences per task. Proofread pass 1 = verb endings and cases; pass 2 = every diacritic.

items.hr: Govorenje: tvrdnja + razlog + primjer, tri rečenice.
items.en: Speaking: statement + reason + example, three sentences.
items.note: Never a one-word answer. Expand every prompt to at least three connected sentences.
An unanswered item scores zero for sure; a reasoned guess might score. Always write something.
Give each section its share and move on. Don't sink ten minutes into one hard item.
prag = threshold. The scored sections need ≥60%; writing and speaking need a pass (zadovoljio). Every section must be passed.

items.en: Take a deep breath before starting.
items.note: Composure is a strategy. A calm start protects the easy points at the top of each section.

items.en: Go back to what you skipped.
items.note: Skip a stubborn item, keep the flow, and return with your spare time — reading and grammar both reward this.
Log your % for listening, reading and grammar next to mock #1 and day 180 — that's your trajectory.

items.hr: Ovo je proba za pravi ispit.
items.en: This is the rehearsal for the real exam.
items.note: Treat it exactly like exam day: no aids, audio twice only, writing by hand, speaking recorded and rubric-graded.

235.title: Timed mixed mock — all five section types
235.intro: A ten-item timed-style mock that touches every section: two listening items, one reading, several grammar structures, and a self-check on writing and speaking. Difficulty climbs 1 to 9. Work quickly and cleanly — then take the FULL five-section mock in the Quiz tab under real conditions.

questions.prompt: Which section demands 'statement + reason + example' in every answer?
questions.explanation: Speaking rewards expanded answers — three connected sentences per prompt: tvrdnja + razlog + primjer.

questions.prompt: LISTENING. Listen. What time does the pharmacy's night shift begin?
questions.explanation: Dežurstvo počinje u dvadeset sati = the night shift begins at 8 p.m. (20:00).
questions.audioText: Poštovani, ljekarna na glavnom trgu radi danju do dvadeset sati, a noćno dežurstvo počinje u dvadeset sati i traje do sedam ujutro.

questions.prompt: LISTENING. From the same message: until what time does the night shift last? (upišite broj)
questions.explanation: traje do sedam ujutro = it lasts until seven in the morning. Verify the second number on the replay.

questions.prompt: READING. 'Gradski muzej otvoren je od utorka do nedjelje. Ponedjeljkom ne radi. Ulaz za studente je besplatan.' — When is the museum closed?
questions.explanation: 'Ponedjeljkom ne radi' — closed on Mondays. Scan for the day, then read the sentence around it.

questions.prompt: SELF-CHECK (writing/speaking). Your životopis has 5 sentences and one missing diacritic. What must you do before marking it a pass?
options: add 2-3 sentences and fix the diacritic
questions.answer: add 2-3 sentences and fix the diacritic
questions.explanation: The task asks for 7-8 sentences and correct spelling incl. diacritics. Both rubric points must be met to pass — then take the full mock in the Quiz tab.

questions.prompt: GRAMMAR. Živim u ______ već pet godina. (Zagreb)
questions.explanation: Static location after 'u' → locative: u Zagrebu.

questions.prompt: GRAMMAR. Putujem na more s ______. (prijatelj)
questions.explanation: s + instrumental for company; soft-consonant masculine takes -em: s prijateljem.

questions.prompt: GRAMMAR (aspect). 'I was reading all evening.' — Cijelu večer sam ______.
questions.explanation: A process over time → imperfective: čitao sam. pročitao sam would mean a completed whole book.

questions.prompt: GRAMMAR. Write futur I: 'We will travel to Split.' — U Split ______ (putovati, mi).
questions.explanation: -ti verb drops -i, auxiliary written separately: putovat ćemo — two words.

questions.prompt: GRAMMAR (clitics). Put in order: 'She gave it to me yesterday.'
questions.explanation: After the fronted Jučer: dative mi → accusative ga → aux je LAST → participle dala. je always closes the cluster.

236.title: Analiza pogrešaka: pretvori promašaj u bod
236.intro: Three data points now show your trajectory: day 180, mock #1 and mock #2. The value is in the analysis. Classify every error, compare with mock #1, and celebrate each category you've fixed — then aim the last four days at whatever still sits under 60%. These frames are the language of a clean error review, and the drill that follows targets the B1 points that most often cost points: clitic order, aspect, passive, conditional and case forms.

items.note: greška iz nepažnje = a careless slip; greška iz neznanja = a knowledge gap. Treat them differently in review.
razvrstati = to sort into categories: cases, aspect, clitics, spelling. A sorted log is a study plan.
Koja se pogreška ponavlja? A recurring error is worth ten one-offs — fix the pattern, not the instance.

items.en: to compare with the previous test
items.note: usporediti + s + instrumental. Line mock #2 up against mock #1: what improved, what stalled?
vidljiv napredak = visible progress. Naming what you fixed keeps motivation high for the final push.
The one section or rule that still drags the score down — that gets tomorrow's focused day.
The fixed sequence: li → aux → dative → accusative → se → je. A classic recurring B1 error worth drilling.
Completed vs ongoing: pročitao sam (finished) vs čitao sam (was reading). The choice is decided by meaning, not by tense.
biti + trpni pridjev (Pismo je napisano) or the se-passive (Ovdje se govori hrvatski). Recognise and form both.
bih/bi/bismo + participle for wishes, polite requests and if-clauses. Watch the plural forms bismo/biste.
The endings after prepositions and numbers — the single biggest source of lost points. Drill the ones your log flags.

items.hr: Prag je jasan: ≥60% × tri + prolaz u pisanju i govorenju.
items.en: The threshold is clear: ≥60% × three + a pass in writing and speaking.
items.note: Check the pass rule section by section. Every section must clear its bar — one fail sinks the whole exam.

236.title: Targeting the recurring B1 errors
236.intro: The five categories that most often show up in an error log: clitic order, aspect in context, the passive, the conditional and case forms. Fix the pattern here so it stops recurring in the real exam.

questions.prompt: A recurring error is more important to fix than a one-off because…
options: it will cost points again and again
questions.answer: it will cost points again and again
questions.explanation: Fix the pattern, not the instance: a pogreška koja se ponavlja costs points on every appearance.

questions.prompt: CASES. Nema više ______ u boci. (voda)
questions.explanation: nema + genitive: voda → vode. Nema vode = there's no water.

questions.prompt: ASPECT. 'Every morning I drink coffee and read the paper.' — Svako jutro pijem kavu i ______ novine.
questions.explanation: A habitual, repeated action → imperfective present: čitam. pročitam would mark a single completed reading.

questions.prompt: CASES. Idem u kino sa ______. (sestra → instrumental)
questions.explanation: s/sa + instrumental for company: sestra → sestrom.

questions.prompt: CONDITIONAL. Choose the standard form: 'We would gladly come.' — Rado ______ došli.
questions.explanation: 1st person plural conditional is bismo, not colloquial 'bi': Rado bismo došli.

questions.prompt: PASSIVE. 'This bridge was built in 1926.' — Ovaj most ______ 1926. godine.
questions.explanation: Passive participle agrees with most (masculine singular): most je sagrađen. Feminine sagrađena, neuter sagrađeno.

questions.prompt: CLITICS. Correct order: 'Have you told him?'
questions.explanation: li follows the first stressed word (Jesi li), then the dative clitic mu, then the participle: Jesi li mu rekao?

questions.prompt: CLITICS. Put in order: 'I bought it for her yesterday.'
questions.explanation: After Jučer: aux sam → dative joj → accusative ga → participle kupio. The aux sam precedes the pronoun clitics (only 'je' goes last).

237.title: Ciljano na najslabiju sekciju
237.intro: Generic review is over — today is surgical. You picked the one section that scored lowest in mock #2, and you drill its task types and its traps until it clears 60% comfortably. This catalogue names the trap in each section so you can aim precisely, then finish with a confidence rep of your strongest section. Whatever your weak spot, the fix is the same: know the trap, drill the pattern.

items.hr: Slušanje: broj čuješ jednom — zapiši ga odmah.
items.en: Listening: you hear the number once — write it down at once.
items.note: The trap is a mistaken digit or time. Note on pass 1, confirm on pass 2. Extra HRT bulletins if this is your weak section.

items.hr: Čitanje: jedna promijenjena riječ ruši TOČNO/NETOČNO.
items.en: Reading: one changed word sinks a true/false item.
items.note: The trap is a near-match sentence. Compare the option word for word with the text before you commit.

items.en: Grammar: the preposition dictates the case.
items.note: The trap is the wrong ending. Learn the preposition-case pairs cold: od/do/iz + gen., u/na/o + loc., s + ins.

items.hr: Pisanje: prekratko ili bez dijakritika = pad.
items.en: Writing: too short or missing diacritics = a fail.
items.note: The trap is stopping at five sentences or skipping a č/ć. Count sentences, then proofread diacritics separately.

items.en: Speaking: a one-word answer = a lost point.
items.note: The trap is a bare da/ne. Expand every prompt: statement + reason + example.
Repetition on the exact task type is what moves the score, not more new material.

items.hr: Vrati se na svoj dnevnik pogrešaka.
items.en: Go back to your error log.
items.note: Every logged entry for the weak section gets re-drilled today until the rule is automatic.
If grammar is weak, drill declension tables aloud — nominative to instrumental, singular and plural.
The goal isn't to scrape the threshold but to clear it with margin, so a bad day still passes.
One confidence rep of your best section ends the day on success — good for morale before exam week.
This late, consolidate what you know. New grammar now only adds noise, not points.
The rule is unforgiving: one section under its bar fails the whole exam. That's why the weak one gets today.

237.title: Surgical drill on the trickiest points
237.intro: A focused set on the highest-value traps across sections: preposition-driven cases, aspect in context, the passive, the conditional and clitic order. Push each until the pattern is automatic and the section clears 60% with margin.

questions.prompt: A one-word answer in the speaking section costs you…
questions.explanation: Examiners reward expansion. A bare da/ne is a lost point — always give statement + reason + example.

questions.prompt: CASES. Knjiga je na ______. (stol)
questions.explanation: na + locative for a static location: stol → stolu. Na stolu = on the table.

questions.prompt: CASES. Vraćam se iz ______ u ponedjeljak. (Rijeka → genitive)
questions.explanation: iz + genitive for motion from: Rijeka → Rijeke. Iz Rijeke = from Rijeka.

questions.prompt: ASPECT. 'She wrote three letters and sent them.' — Napisala je tri pisma i ______ ih.
questions.explanation: A completed single action → perfective poslati: poslala ih je. slala bi would mark a repeated/ongoing action.

questions.prompt: CONDITIONAL. 'Would you help me, please?' (formal, vi) — ______ li mi pomoći, molim vas?
questions.explanation: Polite vi-form conditional: Biste li mi pomogli. bih = I, bismo = we, biste = you (formal/plural).

questions.prompt: PASSIVE. 'The store is opened at eight.' — Trgovina se ______ u osam.
questions.explanation: The se-passive for a habitual action: Trgovina se otvara u osam = the store opens / is opened at eight.

questions.prompt: CASES. Razgovarali smo o ______. (film → locative)
questions.explanation: o + locative: film → filmu. Razgovarali smo o filmu = we talked about the film.

questions.prompt: CLITICS. Put in order: 'Have you already read it to them?'
questions.answer: Jesi li im ga već pročitao
questions.explanation: li after Jesi, then dative im → accusative ga, the adverb već, and the participle: Jesi li im ga već pročitao?

238.title: Pisanje: čisti primjerak svakog žanra
238.intro: Writing is pass/fail, and the bar is modest: one clean, complete, 7-8 sentence text per genre. 'Clean' should be your default, not your best day. This checklist covers the two exam genres — a description (your day, your home, a picture) and a životopis (CV) — plus the two-pass proofreading routine that catches the errors examiners look for. Make each phrase yours, then write both by hand under the clock.

items.note: The exam length. Count them at the end. Six is a risk; four is a fail. Aim for eight clean ones.
Even a short text needs shape: open the topic, develop it, close it. A one-line conclusion lifts the mark.
The set genre: tko ste, odakle ste, obrazovanje, radno iskustvo, jezici, interesi. Perfekt with gender agreement throughout.

items.en: a description (of a day, a home, a picture)
items.note: The other genre. Present tense, plenty of locative (u sobi, na katu) and one reason with jer.
Perfekt agrees with gender: a man writes Rođen sam 1985. godine u…; a woman Rođena sam. The core CV opener.
Male završio, female završila. Završio sam ekonomiju u Zagrebu. Watch the case: u Zagrebu (locative).
Pass 1: verb endings and cases. Pass 2: every č/ć/š/ž/đ. Two separate passes catch far more than one.

items.en: Check the case after every preposition.
items.note: The most common lost point. iz/od/do + genitive, u/na/o + locative for place, s + instrumental.

items.en: Check the participle's gender in the past.
items.note: As a male writer, every past participle ends in -o (radio, bio, išao) — never -la. A frequent slip under time pressure.
Connected text scores above a list of sentences. Weave in at least one jer and one ali/iako.
Better one sentence fewer, fully correct, than a broken eighth. Finish every sentence you start.

items.hr: Ovo su moji predlošci za dan ispita.
items.en: These are my templates for exam day.
items.note: File both clean copies as your reference. On exam day you adapt a known shape — you don't invent from zero.

238.title: Clean writing — the proofreading pass
238.intro: Rehearse the moves that make a text pass: the CV openers with correct gender, the cases after prepositions, the connectors that turn sentences into text, and the diacritic check. Every FILL is strict — proofread as you go.

questions.prompt: How many sentences should each exam writing task have?
questions.explanation: The B1 tasks ask for 7-8 complete sentences. Count them before you stop.

questions.prompt: Which two genres does the exam set?
questions.explanation: The two genres: an opis (of your day/home/a picture) and a životopis (CV).

questions.prompt: CV opener (male writer): '______ sam 1985. godine u Splitu.' (born)
questions.explanation: Perfekt agrees with gender: a man writes Rođen sam; a woman Rođena sam.

questions.prompt: Case check: 'Studirao sam ekonomiju u ______.' (Zagreb)
questions.explanation: u + locative for place: u Zagrebu. A classic proofreading catch in the CV.

questions.prompt: Proofread (male writer): which past participle is correct for a man?
questions.explanation: A male writer's participle ends in -o: radio sam. radila is the feminine form — a frequent slip under time pressure.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is connected text rather than a bare list?
options: Volim sport. Volim glazbu. Volim čitati.
Volim sport, ali najviše volim glazbu jer me opušta.
questions.answer: Volim sport, ali najviše volim glazbu jer me opušta.
questions.explanation: Connectors ali and jer turn separate facts into connected text, which scores above a list of sentences.

questions.prompt: Put the CV sentence in order: 'After my studies I worked in a bank for three years.'
questions.answer: Nakon studija sam radio u banci tri godine
questions.explanation: Nakon + genitive (studija), perfekt radio sam, u banci (locative), tri godine (genitive singular after 3).

questions.prompt: Diacritic check: rewrite with correct spelling — 'Zavrsio sam srednju skolu.' Give the two corrected words separated by a space.
questions.explanation: Pass 2 of proofreading catches the diacritics: Zavrsio → Završio, skolu → školu.

239.title: Priručnik za dan ispita: mirnoća i oporavak
239.intro: You know the content — today locks in composure. A calm exam-day playbook and a set of recovery phrases mean that when a word won't come, you keep talking instead of freezing. These twelve phrases and rules are your safety net: how to buy a second, how to rephrase, how to signal you're correcting yourself. Rehearse them aloud until they feel like reflexes, not emergencies.

items.note: The classic time-buyer. Fills the pause naturally while you find the word — sounds fluent, not stuck.

items.en: I mean, I wanted to say…
items.note: The self-correction signal (male: htio; female: htjela). Shows control: you noticed the slip and fixed it.
A natural way to flag a missing word and keep going, rather than falling silent.

items.en: Let me think for a moment.
items.note: A calm, complete sentence that gives you a breath. Far better than a long empty pause.
When the first phrasing fails, rephrase around it. You don't need the exact word if you can describe the idea.
Another fluent filler (male: rekao; female: rekla). Buys a second while sounding thoughtful.
If you tangle a sentence, restart it cleanly. The examiner would rather hear one clear version.

items.hr: Slušanje: bilježi na prvom, provjeri na drugom.
items.en: Listening: note on the first play, verify on the second.
items.note: Playbook rule: use both plays with a plan — first the fact, then the confirmation.
Playbook rule: names, dates and numbers first; read closely only around the keyword.
Playbook rule: draft the text, count to 7-8 sentences, then two proofreading passes.

items.hr: Ako zapneš, opiši riječ koju ne znaš.
items.en: If you get stuck, describe the word you don't know.
items.note: The core speaking survival skill: 'ona osoba koja liječi ljude' when you can't recall liječnik. Meaning gets through.

items.en: Take a deep breath and smile.
items.note: Composure is trainable. A breath and a smile reset the nerves and the examiner's impression alike.

239.title: Recovery phrases and the exam-day playbook
239.intro: Drill the safety net: the fillers that buy a second, the self-correction signals, the describe-around-it skill, and the one-line rule for each section. These are the phrases that keep you talking when a word won't come.

questions.prompt: A word won't come mid-answer. What is the best move?
options: use a filler and describe the idea
questions.answer: use a filler and describe the idea
questions.explanation: Keep talking: a filler (Kako da to kažem…) plus a description of the idea keeps meaning flowing. Silence loses points.

questions.prompt: What does 'Kako da to kažem…' do for you?
options: buys a second while sounding fluent
questions.answer: buys a second while sounding fluent
questions.explanation: Kako da to kažem… = how should I put this — a natural time-buyer that fills the pause without sounding stuck.

questions.prompt: Complete the self-correction (male speaker): 'Mislim, ______ sam reći nešto drugo.' (wanted)
questions.explanation: Male: htio sam reći; female: htjela sam reći. 'Mislim, htio sam reći…' signals a controlled self-correction.

questions.prompt: You can't recall 'liječnik'. Which describe-around-it strategy works?
questions.explanation: Describe the concept: 'the person who treats people'. Meaning gets through even without the exact noun.

questions.prompt: PLAYBOOK. What is the rule for the listening section?
options: note on the first play, verify on the second
questions.answer: note on the first play, verify on the second
questions.explanation: Use both plays with a plan: first catch the fact, then confirm it on the replay.

questions.prompt: Which phrase best signals you are rephrasing after a failed attempt?
questions.explanation: Drugim riječima = in other words — the signal to rephrase the idea when the first wording didn't work.

questions.prompt: Put the recovery phrase in order: 'Sorry, may I repeat that?'
questions.explanation: Oprostite + mogu li (may I) + ponoviti (repeat). Restart a tangled sentence cleanly instead of pushing through it.

questions.prompt: PLAYBOOK. The correct order for the writing section is…
questions.explanation: Draft the text, count to 7-8 sentences, then two proofreading passes (endings/cases, then diacritics).

239.title: Zadnja simulacija govorenja — opušteno
239.intro: Your final speaking simulation, and this one you simply enjoy. Your wife plays a friendly examiner one last time. Read your lines ('Me') aloud, use a recovery phrase where a word slips, and notice how naturally the long answers come now. Tomorrow you certify readiness.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Zadnja proba prije pravog ispita. Bez pritiska — samo razgovaramo. Jeste li spremni?
lines.en: Last rehearsal before the real exam. No pressure — we're just talking. Are you ready?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Spreman sam i, iskreno, čak se veselim. Puno je mirnije nego prvi put. Da razmislim na trenutak što ću reći.
lines.en: I'm ready and, honestly, I'm even looking forward to it. It's much calmer than the first time. Let me think for a moment about what I'll say.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Recite mi, kako ste se pripremali za ovaj ispit?
lines.en: Tell me, how did you prepare for this exam?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Učio sam svaki dan gotovo godinu dana. Ujutro sam slušao HRT, a navečer sam vježbao gramatiku i pisanje. Iz vlastitog iskustva, redovitost je važnija od duljine — bolje pola sata svaki dan nego pet sati jednom tjedno.
lines.en: I studied almost every day for a year. In the morning I listened to HRT, and in the evening I practised grammar and writing. From my own experience, regularity matters more than length — better half an hour every day than five hours once a week.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A što vam je bilo najteže?
lines.en: And what was hardest for you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Padeži, bez sumnje. Kako da to kažem… promjene nastavaka su me dugo mučile. Drugim riječima, dugo mi nije bilo jasno zašto se ista riječ mijenja u svakoj rečenici. Ali sada mi to dolazi puno prirodnije.
lines.en: The cases, without a doubt. How should I put this… the ending changes troubled me for a long time. In other words, for a long time it wasn't clear to me why the same word changes in every sentence. But now it comes to me much more naturally.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Lijepo ste se izvukli iz te pauze. Za kraj, zašto vam je hrvatski važan?
lines.en: You recovered from that pause nicely. Lastly, why does Croatian matter to you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Zato što je to jezik moje obitelji. Želim razgovarati s tvojim roditeljima bez prevođenja i, kad budemo imali djecu, želim im govoriti na hrvatskom kod kuće. Baš zato mi ovaj ispit znači puno više od papira.
lines.en: Because it's the language of my family. I want to talk with your parents without translating and, when we have children, I want to speak to them in Croatian at home. That's exactly why this exam means far more to me than a piece of paper.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: To je bio najbolji odgovor dosad. Nema više rough spots — spreman si za pravi ispit.
lines.en: That was your best answer yet. No more rough spots — you're ready for the real exam.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Hvala ti što si me slušala svih ovih mjeseci. Sutra bookiramo ispit.
lines.en: Thank you for listening to me all these months. Tomorrow we book the exam.

240.title: Provjera spremnosti i prijava na pravi ispit
240.intro: Two hundred and forty days from the alphabet to this: confirming readiness and booking the official B1 exam. This LEARN is your can-do checklist in Croatian — the milestones you can now tick — plus the concrete booking steps. The certificate (NN 100/2021) is the legal proof of language for long-term residence and the citizenship pathway. Read each line, tick the can-do items honestly, then book.

items.hr: Mogu voditi pravi razgovor o poznatim temama.
items.en: I can hold a real conversation on familiar topics.
items.note: The B1 can-do for spoken interaction: enter unprepared into conversation on family, work, travel and everyday events.

items.hr: Mogu ispričati priču i opisati doživljaje.
items.en: I can tell a story and describe experiences.
items.note: The B1 spoken-production can-do: narrate a plot, describe experiences and give reasons for opinions and plans.

items.hr: Mogu koristiti svih sedam padeža sa sve većom točnošću.
items.en: I can use all seven cases with growing accuracy.
items.note: The core grammar milestone: the full case system, singular and plural, with growing accuracy.

items.hr: Mogu odabrati pravi glagolski vid u prošlosti, sadašnjosti i budućnosti.
items.en: I can choose the right verb aspect in past, present and future.
items.note: Aspect in context — completed vs ongoing — across all three tenses. A defining B1 skill.

items.hr: Mogu napisati povezan tekst: pismo, kratki izvještaj, životopis.
items.en: I can write connected text: a letter, a short report, a CV.
items.note: The B1 writing can-do: connected texts on familiar topics, personal letters describing experiences.

items.hr: Mogu razumjeti glavne točke jasnog standardnog govora.
items.en: I can understand the main points of clear standard speech.
items.note: The B1 listening can-do: main points of clear standard speech and many radio/TV programmes at a slow, clear pace.

items.hr: Prijava: Croaticum (Zagreb), CEHAS ili Riječka kroatistička škola.
items.en: Registration: Croaticum (Zagreb), CEHAS or the Rijeka School of Croatian Studies.
items.note: The authorised institutions. Croaticum is at FFZG (University of Zagreb); licensed adult-education institutions also administer the exam.

items.en: Contact them about 10 days in advance.
items.note: Register ahead of the exam date — roughly ten days — to secure a place and confirm the schedule.
osobna isprava = ID document (osobna iskaznica or putovnica). You register and sit the exam with valid ID.

items.hr: Pravilo prolaza: ≥60% u slušanju, čitanju i gramatici + prolaz u pisanju i govorenju.
items.en: Pass rule: ≥60% in listening, reading and grammar + a pass in writing and speaking.
items.note: Every section must be passed. The three scored sections need at least 60%; writing and speaking need zadovoljio (a pass).

items.hr: Provjeri praktičnosti: datum, cijenu, dokumente, lokaciju.
items.en: Check the practicalities: date, fee, documents, location.
items.note: Confirm the exam date, the fee, which documents to bring, and where to go before the day itself.
The line to say out loud when you pass (male: Uspio sam; female: Uspjela sam). Two hundred and forty days earned it.

240.title: Victory lap — the readiness check
240.intro: A lighter final set: a victory lap over the milestones you've earned and the booking facts you need. Everything here you already know — enjoy proving it. Then open the exam-readiness view in Progress and book the real thing.

questions.prompt: What does 'Uspio sam!' mean?
questions.explanation: Uspio sam! (male) / Uspjela sam! (female) = I did it! / I succeeded! The line for exam day.

questions.prompt: How many cases can you now use with growing accuracy?
questions.explanation: The B1 milestone: all SEVEN cases, singular and plural, with growing accuracy.

questions.prompt: Which institution in Zagreb administers the official B1 exam?
questions.explanation: Croaticum (at FFZG, University of Zagreb), along with CEHAS and the Rijeka School of Croatian Studies.

questions.prompt: Complete the booking step: 'Javi se otprilike ______ dana ranije.' (how many)
questions.explanation: Contact the institution about ten days in advance to register and secure your place.

questions.prompt: The pass rule requires at least what percentage in listening, reading and grammar?
questions.explanation: ≥60% in each of the three scored sections, plus a pass (zadovoljio) in writing and speaking. Every section must pass.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Prijavljujem se s ______ ispravom.' (personal → osobna)
questions.explanation: s + instrumental: s osobnom ispravom = with an ID document. You register with valid ID.

questions.prompt: Put the celebration sentence in order: 'When I pass the exam, I'll celebrate with my family.'
questions.answer: Kad položim ispit, proslavit ću s obitelji
questions.explanation: Kad + present položim (a future time clause), then futur I proslavit ću + s obitelji (instrumental). Two hundred and forty days, well earned.

240.title: Zadnja provjera i slavlje
240.intro: The final conversation of the whole course. Your wife runs one last can-do check, ticks the milestones with you, and celebrates — and together you picture life speaking Croatian with your future children. Read your lines ('Me') aloud. You've earned every word of this.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pa evo nas na kraju. Napravimo zadnju provjeru. Možeš li voditi pravi razgovor na hrvatskom?
lines.en: Well, here we are at the end. Let's do a final check. Can you hold a real conversation in Croatian?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mogu, i to bez prevođenja u glavi. Upravo ga sada vodim s tobom.
lines.en: I can, and without translating in my head. I'm having one with you right now.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Točno! A možeš li ispričati priču i opisati što si doživio?
lines.en: Exactly! And can you tell a story and describe what you've experienced?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mogu. Mogu ti ispričati cijelu ovu godinu — od prvog dana kad nisam znao ni slova, do danas kad se prijavljujem na ispit.
lines.en: I can. I can tell you this whole year — from the first day when I didn't know a single letter, to today when I'm registering for the exam.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Padeži? Sedam njih, u jednini i množini?
lines.en: The cases? All seven of them, singular and plural?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Više me ne plaše. Nisu savršeni, ali koristim ih sa sve većom sigurnošću.
lines.en: They don't scare me anymore. They're not perfect, but I use them with growing confidence.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Onda je odluka jasna. Nazovimo Croaticum i dogovorimo termin za idući tjedan.
lines.en: Then the decision is clear. Let's call Croaticum and arrange a time for next week.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Već imam osobnu iskaznicu pri ruci. Javit ću im se danas — deset dana ranije, kako treba.
lines.en: I already have my ID card at hand. I'll contact them today — ten days in advance, as one should.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Znaš li što me najviše veseli? Jednog dana ćeš ovako razgovarati s našom djecom.
lines.en: Do you know what makes me happiest? One day you'll talk like this with our children.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Baš to mi je bio cilj od početka. Želim im čitati priče na hrvatskom i razumjeti svaku njihovu riječ.
lines.en: That was exactly my goal from the start. I want to read them stories in Croatian and understand every word they say.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bit ćeš divan tata koji im govori na dva jezika. Ponosna sam na tebe.
lines.en: You'll be a wonderful dad who speaks to them in two languages. I'm proud of you.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Uspio sam — mi smo uspjeli. Hvala ti na svakom danu ovog puta. A sad, na ispit!
lines.en: I did it — we did it. Thank you for every day of this journey. And now, to the exam!
